Both are African supreme creators with similar remote/transcendent qualities

roles.igbo_religion: Supreme creator god (Chi-na-eke: God who creates), source of all things, head of the Igbo pantheon

connections.detail: Both are West African supreme creators who are remote and work through intermediary spirits (alusi/orishas)
Both are African supreme creator gods with similar transcendent withdrawal patterns
Both are African supreme creators, though Chukwu is more actively worshipped

roles.igbo_religion: Earth goddess, enforcer of moral law, mother of all crops, most prominent deity in daily Igbo worship

connections.detail: Both are West African earth goddesses central to daily worship and agricultural life
Cross-cultural parallel as earth mother deities foundational to their traditions

roles.akan_religion: Earth goddess, mother of the dead, associated with truth, harvest, and barrenness

connections.detail: Both are West African earth goddesses with parallel roles in agricultural and moral spheres

name: Makemake

name: Tengri

name: Bathala

dateLabel: Ancient Javanese (pre-Hindu period, possibly Bronze Age origins)

name: Lieu Hanh (Mau Lieu Hanh)

connections.detail: Akka Mahadevi was a contemporary and fellow member of Basavanna's Anubhava Mantapa spiritual assembly; one of the most prominent female voices in the vachana movement

name: Umay

name: Ratu Kidul (Nyai Roro Kidul)

name: Snake Goddess

roles.basque_mythology: Supreme earth goddess; personification of Mother Earth, controls weather, dwells in caves and mountaintops, manifests as fireball

roles.mormonism: Second president and prophet; led the Great Migration to Utah; colonizer and territorial governor

roles.jehovahs_witnesses: Founder of the Bible Student movement and first president of the Watch Tower Society

roles.ahmadiyya: Founder, claimed to be the promised Mahdi, Messiah, and subordinate prophet within Islam

roles.tenrikyo: Foundress (Oyasama), became the Shrine of God the Parent (Tenri-O-no-Mikoto)

roles.oomoto: Foundress, received possession-revelations from Ushitora no Konjin; authored sacred texts in automatic writing

connections.detail: Mother-in-law to Onisaburo, who married her daughter and systematized her revelations into organized religion

roles.oomoto: Co-founder and spiritual leader; systematized theology, expanded movement to over 2 million followers

connections.detail: Married Nao's daughter and transformed her small rural following into a massive religious movement

name: Haile Selassie I

roles.rastafari: Central divine/messianic figure, regarded as Jah (God) incarnate or chosen king; Emperor of Ethiopia 1930-1974

aliases: Conquering Lion of the Tribe of Judah

roles.rastafari: Prophetic forerunner, Pan-Africanist whose teachings inspired the Rastafari movement

roles.wicca: Founder of Gardnerian Wicca, published Witchcraft Today (1954) revealing the modern craft to the public

roles.unification_church: Founder and True Father; regarded as the Lord of the Second Advent completing Jesus's unfinished mission

roles.nation_of_islam: Messenger of Allah (Wallace Fard Muhammad); leader 1934-1975, established NOI theology and institutions

roles.nation_of_islam: Minister and national spokesman; dramatically expanded membership through charismatic preaching

connections.detail: Mentored by Elijah Muhammad within NOI, later broke with him over theological and personal disagreements

roles.falun_gong: Founder and sole Master of Falun Dafa; author of Zhuan Falun and all Falun Gong teachings

roles.soka_gakkai: Third president, transformed SGI into a global Buddhist movement; prolific author, peace activist, and educator

roles.heathenry_asatru: First Allsherjargodi (high priest) of Asatrúarfelagid; farmer-poet who led successful effort for official recognition in Iceland

connections.detail: Close friend of Gerald Gardner; both influenced each other's development of seasonal ritual calendars

roles.raelism: Founder and spiritual leader; claims to be the 40th and final prophet sent by the Elohim extraterrestrials
